channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: The Nobel Peace Prize is being announced against a backdrop of conflicts around the world
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize is being awarded Friday against a backdrop of devastating conflicts raging in the world, notably in the Middle East, Ukraine and Sudan.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Zealand ship didn’t sink because its captain was a woman, the ‘appalled’ defense minister says
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — New Zealand's defense minister issued stinging rebukes of what she said were “vile” and “misogynistic” online remarks by “armchair admirals” about the woman captain of a navy ship that ran aground, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy city-hops across Europe, promoting 'victory plan' to allies
item.description: ROME (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was city-hopping across Europe on Thursday to promote a “victory plan” that he said “aims to create the right conditions for a just end to the war" against Russia, detailing the proposals to European allies after a summit with President Joe Biden w
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Navy looks to robots, AI to extend vessel health and maintenance
item.description: Gecko Robotics uses units that observe and record millions of data points, dramatically increasing the amount of data the U.S. Navy can accumulate regarding vessel health.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 22 killed in airstrikes in central Beirut, with Israel also firing on UN peacekeepers
item.description: The air raid on central Beirut — the deadliest in over a year of war — apparently targeted two residential buildings in separate neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: BILAL HUSSEIN, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens wounded, at least 22 killed in airstrikes in Beirut as tensions escalate in Israel conflict with Iran
item.description: At least 22 people were killed and dozens of others left wounded in airstrikes in central Beirut, further escalating Israel’s deadly conflict with Iran-backed Hezbollah militants in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: British YouTuber and rapper Yung Filly is charged with raping and choking a woman in Australia
item.description: PERTH, Australia (AP) — A British YouTuber and rapper known as Yung Filly has been charged with raping and choking a woman in a hotel room following an Australian music performance.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Blinken tells ASEAN the US is worried about China's 'dangerous' actions in disputed sea
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken told Southeast Asian leaders Friday that the U.S. is concerned about China’s “increasingly dangerous and unlawful” activities in the disputed South China Sea during an annual summit meeting, and pledged the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Ng And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A new mayor takes office in southern Mexico after his predecessor was beheaded
item.description: CHILPANCINGO, Mexico (AP) — A new mayor was sworn in Thursday in a city in southern Mexico where his predecessor was killed and beheaded less than a week after taking office.
item.dc:creator: ALEJANDRINO GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mexico sends 660 soldiers, National Guard to protect lime growers suffering extortion by cartels
item.description: Mexico has sent 660 soldiers and militarized National Guard officers to the western state of Michoacan to protect lime growers who complained they were suffering extortion demands by cartels.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rome mayor responds after Macron says he'll fight to keep 'Emily in Paris' in France: 'Netflix does not take orders from heads of state'
item.description: "Emily in Paris" is sparking a très dramatique back-and-forth between the French president and the mayor of Rome, who are battling about the Netflix series potentially bidding adieu to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: After a 2-year investigation, South African president won't be charged over $580K hidden in a couch
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South African President Cyril Ramaphosa won't face criminal charges over a hidden cash scandal that was revealed more than two years ago and led to an investigation by a special police unit, prosecutors said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UN: Israeli military fired on peacekeepers in southern Lebanon
item.description: The United Nations on Thursday accused the Israeli military of firing on peacekeeper missions in southern Lebanon as Israel conducts a cross-border operation inside the country.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump rolls out tax breaks, but no specifics, for overseas citizens and auto buyers
item.description: Donald Trump is rolling out more plans for tax breaks without offering details on how they would work and the impact they would have on the federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Brown votes against student proposal to divest from companies allegedly connected to war in Gaza
item.description: Brown University announced Thursday it will not divest from companies that pro-Palestinian students say contribute to Israel's war efforts in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: UN-backed experts say Israel is destroying Gaza's health sector and both sides have tortured people
item.description: A U.N. commission on Thursday accused Israel of destroying Gaza's health care system through “relentless and deliberate attacks” in its yearlong war with Hamas and said that Palestinian prisoners and Israeli hostages have been tortured and sexually abused.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Israel continues to weigh options, timing on Iran strike following Biden-Netanyahu call
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a call that in part focused on Iran's recent missile attack on the Jewish state, and reportedly how Israel intends to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia’s river guardians battle to protect the Atrato amid threats and abandonment
item.description: PAIMADO, Colombia (AP) — Sediment and pebbles are all that’s left on the earth around much of Bernardino Mosquera’s small riverside community in northwest Colombia’s Choco region.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Archaeologists unearth unexpected find inside a tomb likely belonging to a Roman gladiator
item.description: In Turkey, archaeologists unearthed a tomb seemingly belonging to a Roman gladiator of the third century B.C., but were surprised to find the remains of 12 individuals inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cambodian fishermen turn to raising eels as Tonle Sap lake runs out of fish
item.description: KAMPONG PHLUK, Cambodia (AP) — Em Phat, 53, studies his eel tanks with the intensity of a man gambling with his livelihood.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL and ANTON L. DELGADO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel seeks to reshape Middle East, with force and US backing
item.description: One year after Hamas’s Oct. 7 attack, Israel is reshaping the Middle East by force, taking the fight to Iran and its proxies with the help of the United States — despite pleas from the Biden administration for diplomacy.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: With roads often blocked by rebels in Congo, boats — the only alternative — have become death traps
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The overcrowded boat that capsized in eastern Congo last week killed eight members of Serge Nzonga’s family along with 70 others. Days later, he was back on the same route that claimed their lives in yet another boat lacking safety measures.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Turkey's navy starts to evacuate its citizens from Beirut as Israel battles Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Over 2,000 Turkish citizens and some foreign nationals started boarding a Turkish military ship late Wednesday that brought in aid and will take them out of a country being hit by Israeli airstrikes targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from an AP investigation of Russia recruiting Africans to make drones for use in Ukraine
item.description: About 200 women ages 18-22 from across Africa have been recruited to work in a factory alongside Russian vocational students assembling thousands of Iranian-designed attack drones to be launched into Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and LORI HINNANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelenskyy will present his Ukraine 'victory plan' to Western leaders but Biden meeting is postponed
item.description: DUBROVNIK, Croatia (AP) — Ukraine's president on Wednesday said he intends to present his “victory plan” to Western leaders this week, but a weekend meeting to discuss it has been postponed as U.S. President Joe Biden said he had to stay home to respond to Hurricane Milton’ s landfall in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump 'God Bless the USA' Bibles printed in China, records show
item.description: Thousands of copies of former President Trump's "God Bless the USA" Bible were printed in China, according to trade records reviewed by The Associated Press. According to AP, the records showed a printing company in eastern China shipped nearly 120,000 of the Trump Bibles to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Fugitive father missing with 3 kids since 2021 filmed hiking in New Zealand
item.description: “This was a credible sighting, and police believe it was indeed Tom and his children,” a press release from New Zealand Police said.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: John Legend calls for Harris to put 'conditions' on US aid to Israel
item.description: Music superstar John Legend is calling for Vice President Harris to make U.S. aid to Israel conditional, amid its ongoing offensives in Gaza and Lebanon. In an interview with Zeteo’s Mehdi Hasan, Legend said America must use its aid to Israel as “leverage.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, The Hill

item.title: Macron vows to 'fight hard' to bring Netflix's 'Emily in Paris' back to France
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron is on a diplomatic mission to return "Emily in Paris" back to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired another barrage of rockets into Israel on Tuesday, and the militant group’s acting leader vowed to keep up pressure that has forced tens of thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
